title: Simple Snare
immediate_steps: Find a natural funnel or animal trail (look for tracks, scat, or chewed vegetation)
Use wire, parachute cord inner strands, or strong string to make a loop
Tie the snare securely to a solid anchor (tree or heavy drag log)
Suspend the loop over the trail using small twigs to prop it open
Place the snare at head-height of the target animal (e.g., fist width for a rabbit)
avoid: Do not touch the snare area excessively (human scent deters animals)
Do not use weak cordage that an animal can easily chew through
Do not set snares where you cannot check them daily

title: Figure-4 Deadfall Trap
symptoms: Have access to a heavy rock or log
immediate_steps: Carve three sticks: an upright stick, a diagonal release stick, and a horizontal bait stick
Notch the sticks so they interlock in a '4' shape
Balance a heavy flat rock or log on the diagonal release stick
Place bait on the horizontal stick underneath the weight
When an animal bumps the bait stick, the '4' collapses, dropping the weight
avoid: Do not construct the trap without keeping your hands clear of the falling weight
Do not use a weight that is too light (must crush the target instantly)
